#!/usr/bin/env bash
# resolve-checks.sh — Map classified change surfaces to required verification commands.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/resolve-checks.sh [FILE]
#   ./scripts/classify-changes.sh --base main | ./scripts/resolve-checks.sh
#   echo '{"surfaces":["api-startup","database"]}' | ./scripts/resolve-checks.sh
#
# Input:
#   JSON object with a "surfaces" array (output of classify-changes.sh).
#   Reads from FILE argument or stdin.
#
# Output:
#   JSON object with:
#     "scope"    — highest verification tier: "quick", "standard", or "full"
#     "commands" — deduplicated array of shell commands to run, ordered by scope
#
# Exit codes:
#   0  Success
#   1  Usage error or invalid input
#
# ── Surface-to-Check Mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
#   Surface           Commands                                              Scope
#   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   product-copy      (baseline only)                                       quick
#   search-surface    make api-test-unit                                    standard
#   frontend-shell    cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test        standard
#   api-startup       make api-test-unit, make api-lint                     standard
#   auth-policy       make api-test-unit, make api-lint                     standard
#   logging-privacy   make api-test-unit                                    quick
#   web-performance   cd web && pnpm build                                  quick
#   graphql-schema    make api-generate, make web-schema-sync,              standard
#                     make test-standard
#   database          make test-full                                        full
#   contracts         (baseline only)                                       quick
#   ci-infra          make test-quick                                       quick
#   tools             make kapow-test-unit                                  quick
#
#   Baseline: "make test-quick" is always included when any surface is detected.
#   Scope hierarchy: full > standard > quick (output is the maximum).
#
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

set -euo pipefail

# ── Read input ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if [[ $# -ge 1 && -n "$1" ]]; then
  if [[ ! -f "$1" ]]; then
    echo "Error: file not found: $1" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  INPUT=$(cat "$1")
elif [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then
  INPUT=$(cat)
else
  echo "Error: provide a FILE argument or pipe JSON via stdin" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ -z "$INPUT" ]]; then
  echo "Error: empty input" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# ── Extract surfaces ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Parse the surfaces array from JSON without jq dependency.
# Supports both {"surfaces":["a","b"]} and bare ["a","b"] formats.
extract_surfaces() {
  local json="$1"
  # Strip to just the surfaces array content
  local arr
  if [[ "$json" == "["* ]]; then
    arr="$json"
  else
    # Extract the surfaces array value
    arr=$(echo "$json" | sed -n 's/.*"surfaces" *: *\(\[[^]]*\]\).*/\1/p')
    if [[ -z "$arr" ]]; then
      echo ""
      return
    fi
  fi
  # Extract quoted strings from the array
  echo "$arr" | grep -oE '"[^"]*"' | tr -d '"' || true
}

SURFACES=$(extract_surfaces "$INPUT")

if [[ -z "$SURFACES" ]]; then
  # No surfaces — empty result
  echo '{"scope":"none","commands":[]}'
  exit 0
fi

# ── Map surfaces to commands ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Scope levels: quick=1, standard=2, full=3
MAX_SCOPE=1  # baseline is quick

# Associative array for deduplication (command → 1)
declare -A COMMANDS

# Baseline: always include make test-quick
COMMANDS["make test-quick"]=1

add_command() {
  COMMANDS["$1"]=1
}

set_scope() {
  local level="$1"
  if [[ "$level" -gt "$MAX_SCOPE" ]]; then
    MAX_SCOPE="$level"
  fi
}

while IFS= read -r surface; do
  [[ -z "$surface" ]] && continue

  case "$surface" in
    product-copy)
      # baseline only
      ;;
    search-surface)
      add_command "make api-test-unit"
      set_scope 2
      ;;
    frontend-shell)
      add_command "cd web && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test"
      set_scope 2
      ;;
    api-startup)
      add_command "make api-test-unit"
      add_command "make api-lint"
      set_scope 2
      ;;
    auth-policy)
      add_command "make api-test-unit"
      add_command "make api-lint"
      set_scope 2
      ;;
    logging-privacy)
      add_command "make api-test-unit"
      ;;
    web-performance)
      add_command "cd web && pnpm build"
      ;;
    graphql-schema)
      add_command "make api-generate"
      add_command "make web-schema-sync"
      add_command "make test-standard"
      set_scope 2
      ;;
    database)
      add_command "make test-full"
      set_scope 3
      ;;
    contracts)
      # baseline only
      ;;
    ci-infra)
      add_command "make test-quick"
      ;;
    tools)
      add_command "make kapow-test-unit"
      ;;
    *)
      # Unknown surface — baseline only
      ;;
  esac
done <<< "$SURFACES"

# ── Determine scope name ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
case "$MAX_SCOPE" in
  1) SCOPE="quick" ;;
  2) SCOPE="standard" ;;
  3) SCOPE="full" ;;
esac

# ── Build output JSON ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Order commands by scope: quick commands first, then standard, then full.
# Within each tier, alphabetical.

# Categorize commands into tiers
QUICK_CMDS=()
STANDARD_CMDS=()
FULL_CMDS=()

for cmd in "${!COMMANDS[@]}"; do
  case "$cmd" in
    "make test-quick"|"make kapow-test-unit"|"cd web && pnpm build")
      QUICK_CMDS+=("$cmd") ;;
    "make test-full")
      FULL_CMDS+=("$cmd") ;;
    *)
      STANDARD_CMDS+=("$cmd") ;;
  esac
done

# Sort each tier
IFS=$'\n' QUICK_CMDS=($(sort <<< "${QUICK_CMDS[*]+"${QUICK_CMDS[*]}"}")); unset IFS
IFS=$'\n' STANDARD_CMDS=($(sort <<< "${STANDARD_CMDS[*]+"${STANDARD_CMDS[*]}"}")); unset IFS
IFS=$'\n' FULL_CMDS=($(sort <<< "${FULL_CMDS[*]+"${FULL_CMDS[*]}"}")); unset IFS

# Combine in scope order
ALL_CMDS=()
for cmd in "${QUICK_CMDS[@]}"; do [[ -n "$cmd" ]] && ALL_CMDS+=("$cmd"); done
for cmd in "${STANDARD_CMDS[@]}"; do [[ -n "$cmd" ]] && ALL_CMDS+=("$cmd"); done
for cmd in "${FULL_CMDS[@]}"; do [[ -n "$cmd" ]] && ALL_CMDS+=("$cmd"); done

# Build JSON
json="{\"scope\":\"$SCOPE\",\"commands\":["
first=true
for cmd in "${ALL_CMDS[@]}"; do
  $first || json+=","
  first=false
  json+="\"$cmd\""
done
json+="]}"

echo "$json"

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→# verify-ci.sh — CI-friendly verification entrypoint.
     3→#
     4→# Usage:
     5→#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh [OPTIONS]
     6→#
     7→# Options:
     8→#   --scope SCOPE    Verification scope: merge (default) or release
     9→#   --base REF       Git ref for merge scope (default: auto-detect merge base)
    10→#   --dry-run        Show what would run without executing
    11→#   -h, --help       Show this help
    12→#
    13→# Exit codes:
    14→#   0  All checks passed (or no changes detected)
    15→#   1  One or more checks failed
    16→#
    17→# Scopes:
    18→#   merge    Diff-aware — classifies changes against base ref and runs
    19→#            only the required checks (same pipeline as verify-agent)
    20→#   release  Full validation — runs the complete test/lint/build suite
    21→#            regardless of what changed
    22→#
    23→# Examples:
    24→#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh                          # merge scope, auto base
    25→#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --scope release          # full release checks
    26→#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --base origin/main       # explicit base ref
    27→#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --dry-run                # preview without running
    28→#   make verify-ci                                  # via Makefile
    29→
    30→set -euo pipefail
    31→
    32→# ── Colors (disabled when not a TTY) ─────────────────────────────────────
    33→if [ -t 1 ]; then
    34→  GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
    35→  RED=$'\033[0;31m'
    36→  BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
    37→  BOLD=$'\033[1m'
    38→  DIM=$'\033[2m'
    39→  NC=$'\033[0m'
    40→else
    41→  GREEN="" RED="" BLUE="" BOLD="" DIM="" NC=""
    42→fi
    43→
    44→# ── Defaults ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→SCOPE="merge"
    46→BASE_REF=""
    47→DRY_RUN=false
    48→
    49→# ── Arg parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50→while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    51→  case "$1" in
    52→    --scope)
    53→      SCOPE="$2"
    54→      if [[ "$SCOPE" != "merge" && "$SCOPE" != "release" ]]; then
    55→        echo "${RED}Error: --scope must be 'merge' or 'release', got '$SCOPE'${NC}" >&2
    56→        exit 1
    57→      fi
    58→      shift 2
    59→      ;;
    60→    --base)     BASE_REF="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    61→    --dry-run)  DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
    62→    -h|--help)
    63→      sed -n '2,/^$/p' "$0"
    64→      exit 0
    65→      ;;
    66→    *)
    67→      echo "${RED}Error: unknown option: $1${NC}" >&2
    68→      exit 1
    69→      ;;
    70→  esac
    71→done
    72→
    73→# ── Resolve repo root and script dir ─────────────────────────────────────
    74→REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
    75→SCRIPT_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/scripts"
    76→cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    77→
    78→# ── Header ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    79→echo "${BOLD}── verify-ci ──${NC}"
    80→echo "${DIM}Scope: ${SCOPE}${NC}"
    81→
    82→# ── Resolve commands based on scope ──────────────────────────────────────
    83→COMMANDS=""
    84→RESOLVED_SCOPE=""
    85→
    86→if [[ "$SCOPE" == "release" ]]; then
    87→  # Release scope: full suite, no diffing
    88→  RESOLVED_SCOPE="full"
    89→  COMMANDS="make lint
    90→make web-build
    91→make test-full"
    92→
    93→  echo ""
    94→  echo "${BOLD}Scope:${NC} ${RESOLVED_SCOPE}"
    95→  echo "${BOLD}Commands (3):${NC}"
    96→  echo "  ${DIM}1.${NC} make lint"
    97→  echo "  ${DIM}2.${NC} make web-build"
    98→  echo "  ${DIM}3.${NC} make test-full"
    99→  echo ""
   100→
   101→elif [[ "$SCOPE" == "merge" ]]; then
   102→  # Merge scope: diff-aware via classify → resolve pipeline
   103→  if [[ -z "$BASE_REF" ]]; then
   104→    # Auto-detect merge base against main
   105→    BASE_REF=$(git merge-base HEAD main 2>/dev/null || echo "main")
   106→    echo "${DIM}Base: ${BASE_REF} (auto-detected merge base)${NC}"
   107→  else
   108→    echo "${DIM}Base: ${BASE_REF}${NC}"
   109→  fi
   110→  echo ""
   111→
   112→  CLASSIFICATION=$("$SCRIPT_DIR/classify-changes.sh" --base "$BASE_REF")
   113→
   114→  # Extract surfaces array from JSON
   115→  SURFACES_ARRAY=$(echo "$CLASSIFICATION" | sed 's/.*"surfaces" *: *\(\[[^]]*\]\).*/\1/')
   116→  SURFACES=$(echo "$SURFACES_ARRAY" | grep -oE '"[a-z_-]+"' | tr -d '"' || true)
   117→
   118→  if [[ -z "$SURFACES" ]]; then
   119→    echo "${GREEN}No changes detected against ${BASE_REF}. Nothing to verify.${NC}"
   120→    exit 0
   121→  fi
   122→
   123→  # Resolve required checks
   124→  RESOLUTION=$(echo "$CLASSIFICATION" | "$SCRIPT_DIR/resolve-checks.sh")
   125→
   126→  RESOLVED_SCOPE=$(echo "$RESOLUTION" | grep -oE '"scope" *: *"[a-z]+"' | grep -oE '"[a-z]+"$' | tr -d '"')
   127→  COMMANDS=$(echo "$RESOLUTION" | grep -oE '"commands" *: *\[[^]]*\]' | sed 's/"commands" *: *\[//;s/\]$//' | grep -oE '"[^"]*"' | tr -d '"')
   128→
   129→  # Print summary
   130→  SURFACE_COUNT=$(echo "$SURFACES" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
   131→  COMMAND_COUNT=$(echo "$COMMANDS" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
   132→
   133→  echo "${BOLD}Surfaces detected (${SURFACE_COUNT}):${NC}"
   134→  while IFS= read -r s; do
   135→    echo "  • $s"
   136→  done <<< "$SURFACES"
   137→  echo ""
   138→  echo "${BOLD}Scope:${NC} ${RESOLVED_SCOPE}"
   139→  echo "${BOLD}Commands (${COMMAND_COUNT}):${NC}"
   140→  INDEX=1
   141→  while IFS= read -r cmd; do
   142→    echo "  ${DIM}${INDEX}.${NC} ${cmd}"
   143→    INDEX=$((INDEX + 1))
   144→  done <<< "$COMMANDS"
   145→  echo ""
   146→fi
   147→
   148→# ── Dry run exit ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   149→if $DRY_RUN; then
   150→  echo "${BLUE}Dry run — no commands executed.${NC}"
   151→  exit 0
   152→fi
   153→
   154→# ── Execute checks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   155→PASSED=0
   156→TOTAL=0
   157→COMMAND_COUNT=$(echo "$COMMANDS" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
   158→TMPFILE=$(mktemp)
   159→trap 'rm -f "$TMPFILE"' EXIT
   160→
   161→while IFS= read -r cmd; do
   162→  [[ -z "$cmd" ]] && continue
   163→  TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
   164→
   165→  echo -n "${BOLD}[$TOTAL/$COMMAND_COUNT]${NC} ${cmd} ... "
   166→
   167→  START_TIME=$(date +%s)
   168→  if (eval "$cmd") > "$TMPFILE" 2>&1; then
   169→    ELAPSED=$(( $(date +%s) - START_TIME ))
   170→    echo "${GREEN}PASS${NC} ${DIM}(${ELAPSED}s)${NC}"
   171→    PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
   172→  else
   173→    EXIT_CODE=$?
   174→    ELAPSED=$(( $(date +%s) - START_TIME ))
   175→    echo "${RED}FAIL${NC} (exit ${EXIT_CODE}, ${ELAPSED}s)"
   176→    echo ""
   177→    echo "${RED}${BOLD}── Output ──${NC}"
   178→    cat "$TMPFILE"
   179→    echo ""
   180→    echo "${RED}${BOLD}FAILED:${NC} ${cmd}"
   181→    exit 1
   182→  fi
   183→done <<< "$COMMANDS"
   184→
   185→# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   186→echo ""
   187→echo "${GREEN}${BOLD}All ${PASSED}/${TOTAL} checks passed.${NC} (scope: ${RESOLVED_SCOPE})"
   188→

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-ci.sh — CI-friendly verification entrypoint.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --scope SCOPE    Verification scope: merge (default) or release
#   --base REF       Git ref for merge scope (default: auto-detect merge base)
#   --dry-run        Show what would run without executing
#   -h, --help       Show this help
#
# Exit codes:
#   0  All checks passed (or no changes detected)
#   1  One or more checks failed
#
# Scopes:
#   merge    Diff-aware — classifies changes against base ref and runs
#            only the required checks (same pipeline as verify-agent)
#   release  Full validation — runs the complete test/lint/build suite
#            regardless of what changed
#
# Examples:
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh                          # merge scope, auto base
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --scope release          # full release checks
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --base origin/main       # explicit base ref
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --dry-run                # preview without running
#   make verify-ci                                  # via Makefile

set -euo pipefail

# ── Colors (disabled when not a TTY) ─────────────────────────────────────
if [ -t 1 ]; then
  GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
  RED=$'\033[0;31m'
  BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  BOLD=$'\033[1m'
  DIM=$'\033[2m'
  NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  GREEN="" RED="" BLUE="" BOLD="" DIM="" NC=""
fi

# ── Defaults ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SCOPE="merge"
BASE_REF=""
DRY_RUN=false

# ── Arg parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --scope)
      SCOPE="$2"
      if [[ "$SCOPE" != "merge" && "$SCOPE" != "release" ]]; then
        echo "${RED}Error: --scope must be 'merge' or 'release', got '$SCOPE'${NC}" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      shift 2
      ;;
    --base)     BASE_REF="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --dry-run)  DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
    -h|--help)
      sed -n '2,/^$/p' "$0"
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "${RED}Error: unknown option: $1${NC}" >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
done

# ── Resolve repo root and script dir ─────────────────────────────────────
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
SCRIPT_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/scripts"
cd "$REPO_ROOT"

# ── Header ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "${BOLD}── verify-ci ──${NC}"
echo "${DIM}Scope: ${SCOPE}${NC}"

# ── Resolve commands based on scope ──────────────────────────────────────
COMMANDS=""
RESOLVED_SCOPE=""

if [[ "$SCOPE" == "release" ]]; then
  # Release scope: full suite, no diffing
  RESOLVED_SCOPE="full"
  COMMANDS="make lint
make web-build
make test-full"

  echo ""
  echo "${BOLD}Scope:${NC} ${RESOLVED_SCOPE}"
  echo "${BOLD}Commands (3):${NC}"
  echo "  ${DIM}1.${NC} make lint"
  echo "  ${DIM}2.${NC} make web-build"
  echo "  ${DIM}3.${NC} make test-full"
  echo ""

elif [[ "$SCOPE" == "merge" ]]; then
  # Merge scope: diff-aware via classify → resolve pipeline
  if [[ -z "$BASE_REF" ]]; then
    # Auto-detect merge base against main
    BASE_REF=$(git merge-base HEAD main 2>/dev/null || echo "main")
    echo "${DIM}Base: ${BASE_REF} (auto-detected merge base)${NC}"
  else
    echo "${DIM}Base: ${BASE_REF}${NC}"
  fi
  echo ""

  CLASSIFICATION=$("$SCRIPT_DIR/classify-changes.sh" --base "$BASE_REF")

  # Extract surfaces array from JSON

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-ci.sh — CI-friendly verification entrypoint.
#

#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh [OPTIONS]

#   --scope SCOPE    Verification scope: merge (default) or release

#

#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh                          # merge scope, auto base
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --scope release          # full release checks

#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --dry-run                # preview without running
#   make verify-ci                                  # via Makefile

set -euo pipefail

if [ -t 1 ]; then
  GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
  RED=$'\033[0;31m'
  BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  BOLD=$'\033[1m'
  DIM=$'\033[2m'
  NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  GREEN="" RED="" BLUE="" BOLD="" DIM="" NC=""
fi

# ── Defaults ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SCOPE="merge"
BASE_REF=""
DRY_RUN=false

        exit 1

    *)
      echo "${RED}Error: unknown option: $1${NC}" >&2

  esac
done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# verify-ci.sh — CI-friendly verification entrypoint.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh [OPTIONS]
#
# Options:
#   --scope SCOPE    Verification scope: merge (default), release, or nightly
#   --base REF       Git ref for merge scope (default: auto-detect merge base)
#   --dry-run        Show what would run without executing
#   -h, --help       Show this help
#
# Exit codes:
#   0  All checks passed (or no changes detected)
#   1  One or more checks failed
#
# Scopes:
#   merge    Diff-aware — classifies changes against base ref and runs
#            only the required checks (same pipeline as verify-agent)
#   release  Full validation — runs the complete test/lint/build suite
#            regardless of what changed
#   nightly  Release + security audit + fixture validation. For scheduled
#            CI runs that catch drift not visible in normal development
#
# Examples:
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh                          # merge scope, auto base
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --scope release          # full release checks
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --scope nightly          # release + audit + fixture
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --base origin/main       # explicit base ref
#   ./scripts/verify-ci.sh --dry-run                # preview without running
#   make verify-ci                                  # via Makefile

set -euo pipefail

# ── Colors (disabled when not a TTY) ─────────────────────────────────────
if [ -t 1 ]; then
  GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
  RED=$'\033[0;31m'
  BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  BOLD=$'\033[1m'
  DIM=$'\033[2m'
  NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  GREEN="" RED="" BLUE="" BOLD="" DIM="" NC=""
fi

# ── Defaults ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SCOPE="merge"
BASE_REF=""
DRY_RUN=false

# ── Arg parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --scope)
      SCOPE="$2"
      if [[ "$SCOPE" != "merge" && "$SCOPE" != "release" ]]; then
        echo "${RED}Error: --scope must be 'merge' or 'release', got '$SCOPE'${NC}" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      shift 2
      ;;
    --base)     BASE_REF="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --dry-run)  DRY_RUN=true; shift ;;
    -h|--help)
      sed -n '2,/^$/p' "$0"
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "${RED}Error: unknown option: $1${NC}" >&2
      exit 1
      ;;
  esac
done

# ── Resolve repo root and script dir ─────────────────────────────────────
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
SCRIPT_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/scripts"
cd "$REPO_ROOT"

# ── Header ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo "${BOLD}── verify-ci ──${NC}"
echo "${DIM}Scope: ${SCOPE}${NC}"

# ── Resolve commands based on scope ──────────────────────────────────────
COMMANDS=""
RESOLVED_SCOPE=""

if [[ "$SCOPE" == "release" ]]; then
  # Release scope: full suite, no diffing
  RESOLVED_SCOPE="full"
  COMMANDS="make lint
make web-build
make test-full"

  echo ""
  echo "${BOLD}Scope:${NC} ${RESOLVED_SCOPE}"
  echo "${BOLD}Commands (3):${NC}"
  echo "  ${DIM}1.${NC} make lint"
  echo "  ${DIM}2.${NC} make web-build"
  echo "  ${DIM}3.${NC} make test-full"
  echo ""

elif [[ "$SCOPE" == "merge" ]]; then
  # Merge scope: diff-aware via classify → resolve pipeline
  if [[ -z "$BASE_REF" ]]; then
    # Auto-detect merge base against main
    BASE_REF=$(git merge-base HEAD main 2>/dev/null || echo "main")
    echo "${DIM}Base: ${BASE_REF} (auto-detected merge base)${NC}"
  else
    echo "${DIM}Base: ${BASE_REF}${NC}"
  fi
  echo ""

  CLASSIFICATION=$("$SCRIPT_DIR/classify-changes.sh" --base "$BASE_REF")

  # Extract surfaces array from JSON
  SURFACES_ARRAY=$(echo "$CLASSIFICATION" | sed 's/.*"surfaces" *: *\(\[[^]]*\]\).*/\1/')
  SURFACES=$(echo "$SURFACES_ARRAY" | grep -oE '"[a-z_-]+"' | tr -d '"' || true)

  if [[ -z "$SURFACES" ]]; then
    echo "${GREEN}No changes detected against ${BASE_REF}. Nothing to verify.${NC}"
    exit 0
  fi

  # Resolve required checks
  RESOLUTION=$(echo "$CLASSIFICATION" | "$SCRIPT_DIR/resolve-checks.sh")

  RESOLVED_SCOPE=$(echo "$RESOLUTION" | grep -oE '"scope" *: *"[a-z]+"' | grep -oE '"[a-z]+"$' | tr -d '"')
  COMMANDS=$(echo "$RESOLUTION" | grep -oE '"commands" *: *\[[^]]*\]' | sed 's/"commands" *: *\[//;s/\]$//' | grep -oE '"[^"]*"' | tr -d '"')

  # Print summary
  SURFACE_COUNT=$(echo "$SURFACES" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  COMMAND_COUNT=$(echo "$COMMANDS" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

  echo "${BOLD}Surfaces detected (${SURFACE_COUNT}):${NC}"
  while IFS= read -r s; do
    echo "  • $s"
  done <<< "$SURFACES"
  echo ""
  echo "${BOLD}Scope:${NC} ${RESOLVED_SCOPE}"
  echo "${BOLD}Commands (${COMMAND_COUNT}):${NC}"
  INDEX=1
  while IFS= read -r cmd; do
    echo "  ${DIM}${INDEX}.${NC} ${cmd}"
    INDEX=$((INDEX + 1))
  done <<< "$COMMANDS"
  echo ""
fi

# ── Dry run exit ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if $DRY_RUN; then
  echo "${BLUE}Dry run — no commands executed.${NC}"
  exit 0
fi

# ── Execute checks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PASSED=0
TOTAL=0
COMMAND_COUNT=$(echo "$COMMANDS" | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
TMPFILE=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$TMPFILE"' EXIT

while IFS= read -r cmd; do
  [[ -z "$cmd" ]] && continue
  TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))

  echo -n "${BOLD}[$TOTAL/$COMMAND_COUNT]${NC} ${cmd} ... "

  START_TIME=$(date +%s)
  if (eval "$cmd") > "$TMPFILE" 2>&1; then
    ELAPSED=$(( $(date +%s) - START_TIME ))
    echo "${GREEN}PASS${NC} ${DIM}(${ELAPSED}s)${NC}"
    PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
  else
    EXIT_CODE=$?
    ELAPSED=$(( $(date +%s) - START_TIME ))
    echo "${RED}FAIL${NC} (exit ${EXIT_CODE}, ${ELAPSED}s)"
    echo ""
    echo "${RED}${BOLD}── Output ──${NC}"
    cat "$TMPFILE"
    echo ""
    echo "${RED}${BOLD}FAILED:${NC} ${cmd}"
    exit 1
  fi
done <<< "$COMMANDS"

# ── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo "${GREEN}${BOLD}All ${PASSED}/${TOTAL} checks passed.${NC} (scope: ${RESOLVED_SCOPE})"

Additional Instructions:
# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --status=in_progress` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --status=in_progress  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

     ```tsx
     import { Component, type ErrorInfo, type ReactNode } from 'react'
     import { Button, Container, Stack, Text, Title } from '@mantine/core'
     import { posthog, posthogEnabled } from '@/lib/posthog'

     interface Props { children: ReactNode }
     interface State { hasError: boolean; error: Error | null }

     export class ErrorBoundary extends Component<Props, State> {
       constructor(props: Props) {
         super(props)
         this.state = { hasError: false, error: null }
       }
       static getDerivedStateFromError(error: Error): State {
         return { hasError: true, error }
       }
       componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) {
         if (posthogEnabled) {
           posthog.capture('$exception', {
             $exception_message: error.message,
             $exception_type: error.name,
             $exception_stack_trace_raw: error.stack,
             $exception_source: 'react_error_boundary',
             $exception_component_stack: errorInfo.componentStack,
           })
         }
       }
       handleReload = () => { window.location.reload() }
       render() {
         if (this.state.hasError) {
           return (
             <Container size="sm" py="xl">
               <Stack align="center" gap="md">
                 <Title order={2} c="brand.9">Something went wrong</Title>
                 <Text c="dimmed" ta="center">An unexpected error occurred. Please try reloading the page.</Text>
                 <Button color="accent" onClick={this.handleReload}>Reload page</Button>
               </Stack>
             </Container>
           )
         }
         return this.props.children
       }
     }

     import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react'
     import { useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'
     import { posthog, posthogEnabled } from '@/lib/posthog'
     import { useAuthStore } from '@/stores/authStore'

     export function usePostHogPageview() {
       const { pathname } = useLocation()
       useEffect(() => {
         if (!posthogEnabled) return
         posthog.capture('$pageview', { $current_url: window.location.href })
       }, [pathname])
     }

     export function usePostHogIdentify() {
       const user = useAuthStore((s) => s.user)
       const identifiedRef = useRef<string | null>(null)
       useEffect(() => {
         if (!posthogEnabled) return
         if (user && identifiedRef.current !== user.id) {
           posthog.identify(user.id, { email: user.email })
           identifiedRef.current = user.id
         } else if (!user && identifiedRef.current) {
           posthog.reset()
           identifiedRef.current = null
         }
       }, [user])
     }
     ```

     ```go
     package posthog

     import (
       "context"
       "log/slog"
       ph "github.com/posthog/posthog-go"
     )

     type Client struct { inner ph.Client }

     func New(apiKey, host string) (*Client, error) {
       if apiKey == "" {
         slog.Info("posthog: disabled (POSTHOG_API_KEY not set)")
         return &Client{}, nil
       }
       endpoint := host
       if endpoint == "" { endpoint = ph.DefaultEndpoint }
       inner, err := ph.NewWithConfig(apiKey, ph.Config{Endpoint: endpoint})
       if err != nil { return nil, err }
       slog.Info("posthog: initialized", "endpoint", endpoint)
       return &Client{inner: inner}, nil
     }

     func (c *Client) Enabled() bool { return c.inner != nil }
     func (c *Client) Inner() ph.Client { return c.inner }

     func (c *Client) WrapSlogHandler(h slog.Handler) slog.Handler {
       if c.inner == nil { return h }
       return ph.NewSlogCaptureHandler(h, c.inner,
         ph.WithDistinctIDFn(func(_ context.Context, _ slog.Record) string {
           return "kapow-api"
         }),
       )
     }

     func (c *Client) Close() error {
       if c.inner == nil { return nil }
       return c.inner.Close()
     }
     ```
